briefs.title: BC Pharmacies Banned from Charging Patients for Fully Covered Drugs Starting March 1
briefs.summary: Effective March 1, 2026, BC amended three pharmacy regulations: a new drug plan called 'Plan NP' is created for all BC medicare (public health insurance) beneficiaries; insulin is no longer exempt from drug price rules; and pharmacies are prohibited from charging patients any amount for drugs or services that are fully covered by a provincial drug plan, except nutritional vitamins or supplements for people with cystic fibrosis.
briefs.action_required: Review your pharmacy billing practices and remove any extra charges for fully covered drugs before March 1, 2026.

key_dates.label: BC Drug Plans/Price/Provider Regulation amendments take effect
